methodsAttempted.query: site:royalholloway.ac.uk AI policy students artificial intelligence assessment Royal Holloway
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Royal Holloway student generative AI guidance, misconduct guidance, student AI-allegation PDFs, and academic misconduct regulations.

methodsAttempted.query: site:royalholloway.ac.uk generative AI teaching guidance Royal Holloway staff assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the student guidance cluster, including AI Use & Assessment, AI Tools, referencing, and misconduct pages. No staff-wide binding AI teaching policy was verified.

methodsAttempted.query: site:royalholloway.ac.uk Royal Holloway AI research guidance generative AI ethics
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Doctoral School guidance for postgraduate research and a linked briefing PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:royalholloway.ac.uk Royal Holloway artificial intelligence data protection AI tools
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the AI Tools page and a jobs-site PDF; the jobs-site PDF was rejected as applicant-facing recruitment guidance rather than student or institutional academic policy.
Official PDFs for academic misconduct regulations and AI-related allegations were fetched with Firecrawl PDF parsing.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery verified a Royal Holloway student generative AI guidance cluster, official academic misconduct regulations and AI-allegation guidance, and Doctoral School guidance for postgraduate research. Searches also found applicant-facing AI recruitment guidance, research showcase/course-marketing pages, and general AI programme pages, which were rejected as not policy evidence. No official staff-wide binding AI teaching policy was verified in this crawl.

stopConditions: Stop after official Royal Holloway AI guidance, misconduct regulations, PGR guidance, and relevant official PDFs have been fetched.
Do not use third-party summaries or marketing/research showcase pages as policy evidence.
Do not bypass login, paywall, CAPTCHA, robots, MFA, or WAF restrictions.

artifacts.claimText: Royal Holloway tells Foundation, undergraduate, and postgraduate taught students that module assessment briefs take precedence for AI use; if a brief says AI is not permitted, students must not use it and must declare any AI use clearly and honestly.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance on the Use of Generative AI - Royal Holloway Student Intranet
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This guidance provides a broad overview, but all students must follow specific instructions in module assessment briefs as local guidance always takes precedence. If the brief says AI is not permitted, you must not use it. You must declare any AI use clearly and honestly.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Tools - Royal Holloway Student Intranet
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: From the many generative AI tools, Royal Holloway recommends that you use Microsoft Copilot, logging in with your Royal Holloway account details. Please be extremely careful when entering data into a generative AI tool: this is particularly important if you are using one other than MS Copilot and your RHUL login.

artifacts.claimText: Royal Holloway academic misconduct regulations list requesting or engaging an artificial intelligence tool to write or rewrite work for unfair advantage, and presenting AI-generated content as one’s own unless authorised and acknowledged, as examples of assessment offences.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Regulations on Academic Misconduct 2025/26
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Commissioning, which is requesting or engaging another person or artificial intelligence tool whether paid or unpaid to write or rewrite work in order to obtain an unfair advantage for oneself. Presenting content generated by artificial intelligence tools as your own unless specifically authorised in writing as part of the assessment brief and appropriately acknowledged.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Referencing the Use of AI - Royal Holloway Student Intranet
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Explicit acknowledgment of use of generative AI tools such as Large Language Models like Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini or ChatGPT or any other media generated through similar tools is required. If you have not used any such tools in any way a declaration statement is still required. Prompt List: A list of all prompts entered into any large language model must be provided.

If you have not used any such tools in any way a declaration statement is still required. Prompt List: A list of all prompts entered into any large language model must be provided.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance on the Use of Generative AI in Postgraduate Research - Royal Holloway Doctoral School
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Originality: AI may not author or generate research claims, thesis prose or other academic content. artifacts.title: Royal Holloway, University of London AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for Royal Holloway found student generative AI guidance, academic misconduct regulations, AI declaration guidance, approved/recommended Copilot guidance, and postgraduate research guidance. Rejected sources were applicant-facing, research-showcase, or programme-marketing pages and were not used as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No staff-wide binding AI teaching policy was verified in this crawl; claims are scoped to the official sources cited.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
